title: Memory Analysis
description: Techniques for reading, scanning, and interpreting game memory.

topics.title: Reading Memory
topics.description: Safe and effective memory reading techniques.
topics.explanation:
ReadProcessMemory (external) or direct pointer dereference (internal/injected). Alignment matters for multi-byte types. Endianness: x86/x64 is little-endian.

Document values at rest vs during gameplay — many game values are transient.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Reading Memory
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Reading Memory in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Memory Layouts
topics.description: How game data is organized in memory.
topics.explanation:
Typical layout:
- Global singletons in .data
- Entity arrays/pools on heap
- Stack locals per frame
- GPU resources in separate heaps

Draw memory maps linking classes to regions.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Memory Layouts
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Create a memory map diagram for a simple game's player entity

topics.title: Memory Scanning
topics.explanation:
Cheat Engine scan types:
- Exact value
- Unknown initial → changed/unchanged
- Increased/decreased
- Fuzzy (float tolerance)

Narrow results by changing game state between scans.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Memory Scanning
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Memory Scanning in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Pattern Scanning
topics.explanation:
Scan executable regions for byte patterns with wildcards. Account for ASLR by scanning module-relative offsets.

`48 8B 05 ?? ?? ?? ?? 48 85 C0` — common mov rax, [rip+rel]; test rax, rax pattern.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Pattern Scanning

Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Pointer Chains
topics.explanation:
Static base + offsets → dynamic heap address:
`game.exe+0x1234568` → `[+0x20]` → `[+0x18]` → health

Pointer scan in CE finds stable chains across restarts (module-relative bases).
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Pointer Chains

topics.title: Structure Analysis
topics.description: Reverse engineering C++ structures from memory.
topics.explanation:
Workflow:
1. Find base address of object
2. Dump surrounding memory
3. Identify vtable pointer, member fields
4. Correlate changes with game actions
5. Define struct in CE/ReClass.NET

Tools: ReClass.NET, Ghidra struct editor.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Structure Analysis

topics.title: Run-Time Type Information
topics.explanation:
MSVC RTTI includes TypeDescriptor, ClassHierarchyDescriptor. Scanners locate vtables and type names.

Useful for identifying unknown objects in engine binaries. Tools: ClassInformer (IDA), Ghidra RTTI scripts.
